business.headline: TCS Q4 results: 5 key takeaways from a steady quarterThe IT major reported a net profit of Rs 13,718 crore for Q4FY26, up 12.2% year-on-year, while revenue came in at Rs 70,698 crore.

Live: Starmer and Trump discuss the need for a practical plan to restore global shipping flowUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: US President Donald Trump's push to steady the Middle East crisis is colliding with hard realities on the ground, as calls for restraint and negotiations struggle to match the pace of military action. The US has quietly urged Israel to tone down its campaign and move towards dialogue, with Trump emphasising a more "low-key" approach even as signals from Tel Aviv remain mixed. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has opened the door to talks, but continues to maintain that operations against Hezbollah will not pause. The fallout has been severe, with more than 300 people killed in Lebanon. At the same time, Beirut has begun indicating a shift in its own stance, with moves targeting armed groups seen as part of a broader attempt to align with diplomatic efforts. Iran has responded with sharp warnings, arguing that ongoing attacks undermine the very basis of negotiations and risk collapsing an already fragile truce. In a defiant statement, Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei said on Thursday that Iran would be "resolute in avenging" the deaths of his father Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and the country's "martyrs" and "will take management of the Strait of Hormuz into a new phase." Meanwhile, the United States is preparing for crucial talks in Pakistan, which will be held on April 11. Catch all the live updates at Indiatoday.in.

Heartbreak at Eden: Mukul Choudhary's epic heist seals KKR's fate in KolkataKKR vs LSG: An epic rescue act from Mukul Choudhary helped Lucknow Super Giants snatch victory from Kolkata Knight Riders on Thursday. KKR lost their third game of the season, and remain winless in IPL 2026.

Video: Fire breaks out at Mumbai airport, plumes of smoke fill premisesThe blaze, which caused plumes of smoke, was confined to the ground floor of a terminal structure and brought under control within minutes.

Pakistanis flex peacemaker tag, get roastedFragile as the US-Iran ceasefire may be, it still hasn't stopped the average Pakistani user on X from rushing to claim the crown of the world's peacemaker. Highlights: Iran to avenge late Supreme Leader, martyrs, says Mojtaba KhameneiUS-Israel-Iran War Highlights: The Middle East slipped back into instability as Lebanon witnessed some of the heaviest attacks by Israel despite a two-week truce with the US, with over 200 people killed and around 1,000 injured. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu made it clear that Lebanon is not part of the agreement; even as the United States and Iran announced a pause in attacks, fresh strikes were reported across Iran, the UAE, Kuwait and Bahrain, raising serious doubts over the truce's durability, while US President Donald Trump said the ceasefire — brokered with Pakistan's involvement — includes Tehran allowing safe transit through the Strait of Hormuz, with talks scheduled in Islamabad on Friday to decide the next steps. Iran has claimed victory but stressed that the upcoming negotiations do not signal the end of the war. Trump indicated possible sanctions and tariff relief for Tehran while reiterating that Iran will not be allowed to enrich uranium and warning of steep tariffs on countries supplying weapons to Iran, even as oil prices eased following the announcement, with Brent crude slipping below $95 a barrel. Catch all the live updates at Indiatoday.in.

No more than 15 ships daily: Iran sets traffic limit for Strait of HormuzIran will cap traffic through the Strait of Hormuz at 15 vessels per day under a ceasefire deal with the United States, after months of disruption that had driven up global oil prices.

I am not Bitcoin creator Satoshi, says Adam Back as he refutes NYT evidenceAdam Back has strongly pushed back against fresh claims that he is Bitcoin creator Satoshi Nakamoto after an NYT report claimed that he is.
